intro: The wrist is a complex joint made of many small bones gliding against each other.
notes: Carpals: 8 small bones arranged in two rows.
Gliding: Allows the hand to wave and circle.
Connects the forearm to the hand.

hotspots.label: Radius
hotspots.description: The wide end of the Radius connects to the Carpal bones to form the main wrist joint.
hotspots.examTip: The Ulna does not actually touch the wrist bones directly (there is a disc in between).

hotspots.label: Carpals
hotspots.description: These 8 pebble-like bones slide over each other to allow the wrist to bend, straighten, and move side-to-side.
hotspots.examTip: This gliding motion is what makes the wrist so flexible.

hotspots.label: Metacarpals
hotspots.description: These bones connect the wrist (Carpals) to the fingers (Phalanges).
hotspots.examTip: The joints between Carpals and Metacarpals are also Gliding joints (except for the thumb).
